title: Ageratina modesta
intro: Ageratina modesta, also known as modest ageratina or modest mountain silver, is a perennial plant species in the aster family, native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Ageratina modesta include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as 11β-hydroxy-11α,13-dihydroageratoic acid and 11β-hydroxyageratoic acid. Ageratina modesta is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Ageratina modesta is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant belongs to the genus Ageratina, which is a group of flowering plants. The genus Ageratina is further grouped into the tribe Eupatorieae, which contains numerous species of plants that resemble each other in appearance. Ageratina modesta is native to the Americas and is known to thrive in a variety of environments. Its classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic makeup.
